title: Prestige Override
description: Schema for correcting tarkov.dev prestige-level data. Keyed by tarkov.dev prestige ID. Each entry patches fields on the matching item of the API `prestige` array; `conditions` is an ID-keyed object of per-condition patches or overlay-only synthetic conditions, and `storyRequirements` is the authoritative list of story-chapter requirements shown in-game.

prestigeLevel.description: Prestige level this entry corresponds to (documentation/sanity aid; must match the API value)

name.description: Corrected display name for the prestige level

conditions.description: Per-condition corrections, keyed by condition ID. Keys matching tarkov.dev condition IDs patch existing conditions; overlay-only keys are synthetic conditions consumers should append.

type.description: Condition type (e.g. taskStatus, playerLevel, skill, hideoutStation, haveItem)

task.description: Corrected task reference for a taskStatus condition (tarkov.dev task ID, or an overlay tasksAdd id like new_beginning_prestige_5)

status.description: Corrected status array for a taskStatus condition

playerLevel.description: Corrected player level for a playerLevel condition

skill.description: Skill name for a skill condition

level.description: Corrected level for a skill condition

storyRequirements.description: Authoritative story-chapter requirements shown by the in-game prestige screen. Empty array means no story requirement.

type.description: Whether the prestige requires a whole story chapter or one objective inside it

storyChapter.description: storyChapters id, e.g. tour or the-ticket

objective.description: Objective id inside the story chapter when type is storyObjectiveStatus

name.description: Display name shown by the in-game prestige screen

status.description: Required status for the story chapter/objective
